article.title: DEA finalizes update to Electronic Prescriptions for Controlled Substances rule
article.summary: DEA's final rule under 21 CFR 1311 updates EPCS technical requirements, including biometric authentication options for the second factor and a 1-year audit log retention floor. Effective 90 days from publication.
article.rawContent: The Drug Enforcement Administration has finalized amendments to the EPCS rule (21 CFR 1311). Key changes: (1) biometric authentication (fingerprint, facial recognition meeting NIST SP 800-63 Level 2) is now an explicit second-factor option alongside hard tokens and authenticator apps; (2) audit log retention floor raised from 6 months to 1 year for prescription records; (3) annual EPCS application identity-proofing renewal clarified as required for individual providers, not separately for each practice; (4) DEA registration renewals for prescribers must reaffirm EPCS-only-prescribing capability or document a written exception. Effective 90 days from publication.
